name: Innate
description: Innate builds AI brains for general-purpose robots, and affordable embodiments for consumers to experience the first AI robots. Our first product, Maurice, is a mobile robot with a trainable arm, designed for Silicon Valley builders to play with, customize and build on. It runs our AI brain that can navigate, perform physical tasks, interact with people, and reason. https://youtu.be/gxRLFx4JjcQ The brain is completely teachable: You can prompt it, chat with it, and teach it physical tasks. It takes only 5 minutes for you to write another prompt for Maurice to behave very differently, for example, performing security at home: https://youtu.be/hGK0lIGT49E In the past 3 months, we created our brain, an AI orchestrator leveraging several of the most recent research publications in AI for manipulation, navigation, reasoning and planning. It combines 9 different models to create something that feels innately intelligent - like GPT, Gemini, Claude and Llama (Groq) got a body and learned to move. Maurice constantly understands its environment, makes decisions, remembers what is sees and where, and learns from you. This system comprises: 🧠 A multi-agent orchestrator running specialized models locally and in cloud 🤖 Real-time manipulation models controlled by our own transformers 🦾 A full data pipeline to collect arm data, train in the cloud, run inference on the platform 💬 A 100% interactive platform, that can be steered vocally or through its prompts 📈 An eval pipeline for our brain’s decision making, to progressively make it better Maurice learns your space, remembers context, and makes autonomous decisions about where, what to do next. It’s also ready to build on: All the compute required is onboard (uses a Jetson Orin Nano) Shipped with a teleop arm to train it quickly SDK to access the agent, steer it, give it new functions to call And as a user, you can customize it, collect data quickly to train it, and even change the hardware. This allows Maurice (and future robots) to learn several kinds of physical tasks. Finally, Maurice is already reliable enough for us to do live, autonomous demos! We're also developing a larger robot that can reach countertops. Our interoperable software ensures quick capability matching across all future embodiments using the Innate brain. We're making teachable robots accessible to builders now, because we believe it is the right way to tailor robots people want, for a reasonable price. Furthermore, the data we collect along the way, teaching our robots, will be useful for all future robots. Our first platform is for Silicon Valley builders who want to shape what teachable robots can do. Sign up for our waitlist on our website. We are Vignesh and Axel, two former Stanford grad students and researchers in AI for robotics and AI for HCI. We met almost a year ago working on a previous idea, and through several iterations of this project, we have come to trust our shared abilities to face the hard problems ahead to make general-purpose robotics accessible to everyone. This company embodies our vision of bringing a technology that will be a major game-changer for humanity, in the hands of everyone. So that robots are made for humans, taught by humans. Us, 10mn before a successful live demo of our robot in front of 40 people, fixing an unexpected bug The future of robotics is teachable. It’s time to build it!

name: InQuery
description:
**TLDR: InQuery helps carriers, state funds, self-insured employers and TPAs automate tedious parts of the claims processing workflow so that claims can get closed faster and litigated less.**

Hey everyone, we’re Erick and Khalil and we’re building InQuery.

🔍 Problem
The insurance industry is facing a labor shortage that is creating a negative spiral for staffing. As carriers struggle to hire, existing talent is getting overloaded and under-trained, causing severe retention issues at most large carriers. This talent squeeze means that per-case performance quality drops, leading claimants to experience confusion and frustration, which inevitably drives litigation rates upwards as they seek representation and support. As a result, average claim costs are rising, squeezing carriers expense ratios at the same time as inflation causes ballooning medical costs. Still, claims adjusters like my sister spend most of their day transcribing telephone calls, navigating large sets of semi-structured policies, and mapping data from large and complicated medical documents to relatively structured schemas in their existing CMS. 🦾 Our Solution At InQuery, our mission is to develop AI agents tailored for the insurance industry, beginning with with claims handling. These agents will enable claims handlers to concentrate on high-impact settlement strategies, effective negotiations, and casualty remediation processes. The result? Reduced litigation rates, improved loss ratios, and enhanced satisfaction for both employees and claimants. But our vision doesn’t stop there. As we perfect our AI solutions for claims handling, we’re poised to expand into other insurance-specific workflows, bringing efficiency and innovation to every corner of the industry. Our AI agents will seamlessly integrate with your current team, taking over repetitive tasks and collaborating with human staff where their expertise is needed most. Join us in redefining insurance processes and empowering your workforce to achieve more.

**👋 About us:**
Before starting InQuery, we’ve been best friends since living in the same dorm during our freshman year studying Computer Science at Stanford. Our Ask
If you or anyone you know has experienced the pain of handling claim caseloads of upwards 100+ active cases, we’d love to chat. We’re looking for intros to carriers, self-insured employers, and TPAs.

Even if our product isn’t the right fit for you yet, we’d love to learn about what’s working well and what’s broken at your organization and share what we’ve learned from talking to dozens of carriers ourselves.

name: InspectMind AI
description:
Every construction project is slowed down by drawings mistakes: These inconsistencies cause rework, schedule delays, and frustrated clients. They happen on every project, and can cost 6-7 figures per job. Humans aren’t the best to check hundreds or thousands of pages. An AI agent analyzes all project documents, architectural / structural / civil / MEP drawings, project specifications, and building codes, and flag issues like dimension mismatches, materials mismatches, ownership/responsibility confusion, missing info, ADA/code red flags, and uncoordinated addenda. It’s simple: you upload your project documents in PDF. DONE. AI will prepare the issues list that is easy to review in our web app. No workflow change. No training. Just fewer errors and better margins. We’re already running our agent on Tenant Improvement, K-12, civic, industrial, healthcare, affordable housing, and residential projects. It recently saved a GC $2M in rework for a $100M project due to a material mismatch. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mm24TApb8bg General Contractors, developers, architects, engineers, and owners: Want us to run Checker on your next project? Email me directly: aakash@inspectmind.ai Aakash & Shuangling
InspectMind AI (YC W24)

name: Inventive AI
description: Inventive is on a mission to drive 10x productivity in sales workflows with AI Agents. Our product, the Inventive platform, enables sales teams to respond to RFPs and Sales Questionnaires in a fraction of the time. See a short video here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dZdaD_1zo8Q In the US, projects > $1 Trillion get allocated via RFPs. in public and private domains across technology, defense, construction & more. But responding to RFPs is daunting and challenging Inventive provides a series of agents and automation that enable sales teams to respond to RFPs and sales questionnaires 70% faster. 10X Faster Drafts With Highly Accurate Responses: Inventive's proprietary AI models provide a first draft of RFP responses 10X faster. These responses are accurate, reliable, and rooted in the organization’s sources. Single Hub for All Your Knowledge Sources: Inventive integrates with many of your enterprise platforms, curating a single source of truth for all your proposal and pre-sales assets and reducing the dependency on SMEs. Fight Conflicting Content With AI Content Manager: Inventive’s AI Content Manager proactively flags stale and conflicting content at scale, saving proposal teams a lot of time and manual effort. AI Agents for Productivity & Competitive Advantage: The Inventive platform has a number of AI Agents focused on strategic tasks and can help users with researching competitors, brainstorming creative ideas, and managing compliance. “Inventive has been a game changer for us. We have been 3X more productive.” “Our Solutions Engineering team has experienced remarkable improvements in speed and accuracy with Inventive.” “We are being down selected and winning more RFPs than ever before!” Book a free demo at http://www.inventive.ai/demo

name: Inviscid AI
description:
TL;DR: We are creating real-time physics simulations & digital twins of physical spaces. 1000x faster than traditional solvers; fast enough to actually optimize your energy costs, design cycles, and live operations. Building operations account for approximately 40% of total energy consumption in the United States and the European Union. Of that, HVAC alone eats up roughly 40%,  making heating and cooling the single largest energy end-use in the built environment. And yet, the physics tools to understand and optimize these systems in real time have never existed. Traditional CFD solvers take 8-12 hours per simulation. That's fine for final-stage validation. But it means you can never use simulation to answer questions like: These are real-time decisions. You can't wait 12 hours for them. So today, operators guess, over-provision, and leave massive efficiency gains on the table. Not because they don't care, but because the physics was always too slow to be useful at the moment. We fix that. Our engine runs ~1000x faster than traditional solvers using GPU accelerated numerical solvers or physics-informed neural operators, fast enough to turn simulation from a one-off analysis into a live, continuous control layer over physical systems. We've achieved state-of-the-art performance on standard Navier-Stokes benchmarks across multiple physics domains. This isn't a cheaper simulation. It's a fundamentally new capability: real-time physics-aware control of the physical world. We built InviscidAI to power real-time decisions across any domain where physics matters: HVAC and building operations are our starting point. Our end goal is to model all fields of physics - fluid dynamics, thermodynamics, electromagnetics, structural mechanics, and beyond - and deliver real-time digital twins for any physical system. We want every engineer, operator, and designer to have a live, physics-accurate model of whatever they're building or managing. If it obeys the laws of physics, we want to simulate it in real time. We're deploying custom digital twins for enterprise clients. We're looking for introductions to: If you have a physics problem that you want to solve faster, we want to hear about it. Book a time here: https://calendly.com/kabir-inviscidai/30min 📧 kabir@inviscidai.com | 📞 +1(408) 303-9627 | 🌐 www.inviscidai.com

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ISM5gMWZQr4

name: Invofox
description: tl;dr: We help software companies automatically extract data from invoices, so they can fully automate processes for their customers, such as payments or accounting, without anyone having to review the data. Reach out through our website to learn more! Hi everyone, we’re Adrian, Alberto and Carmelo, and we are the founders of Invofox. We met at university, in Spain, while the three of us majored in math and computer science. From left to right: Adrian is our machine learning expert. He’s responsible for the deep tech side of Invofox. Chat with him about #machineLearning #artificialIntelligence #bouldering or #beerBrewing Alberto is our CEO. He’s responsible for business development and investors relations. Chat with him about #b2bSales #outboundSales #literature or #crossfit Carmelo is our CTO. He’s responsible for the productization of our technology, making it usable for our customers. Chat with him about #javaScript #mongoDB #boardGames or #parkour Well, no. The first time we approached this problem we thought the same thing, but it really isn’t. Most commercial solutions rely on one of two approaches: Every year 100+ billion invoices are issued in the USA and EU, wasting tens of billions of dollars in errors and unproductive costs. Invofox allows you to automate all of the processes taking place around invoices, such as payments or accounting, without anyone having to review those documents. We do this with an easy to use, stripe like, API, that allows software companies to seemlessly integrate our technology into their product and provide the best possible service to their customers. Our quick blurb: Invofox helps software companies automatically extract data from invoices, so they can fully automate processes for their customers, such as payments or accounting, without anyone having to review the data. We process 17+ million invoices per year, mostly in Europe, and we are currently expanding to the US, Canada, Mexico and India.

name: ion design
description: We want you to meet Ruby. tl;dr: You can assign Ruby design tasks right in slack, like optimizing a flow or creating a new page. She clarifies requirements, researches, designs multiple options, iterates with you, and ships PRs to your codebase. She will then monitor performance and suggest future improvements. She’s like Devin, but for Product Design work. 2 min demo - https://youtu.be/0MccwmsZOOc?si=FqSIPUbiX-Y160h8 “Over the next decade, as new coding tools make it easier than ever to build and ship products quickly, great design is going to matter even more.” - Aaron Epstein, latest YC RFS We agree, and good design takes a LOT of time, money, and care. Ruby can leverage your design thinking and execute against it 100x faster. Whenever you build a flow, you can be sure Ruby will make it as good as it can be. When assigned a task, ruby works through a flow to solve the problem: Our team was early in generative code & design. I’ve been shipping ai design-to-code tools since 2021, and we recently built an AI visual builder called ion. Internally, we trained an AI that uses our visual builder itself, and taught it the design process. It blew us away. We named her Ruby, put her into slack, and started giving her to our customers. Her results look promising so we’re sharing her more 🙂 Ruby is meant for fast moving startups who are serious about getting product changes in the hands of customers faster. If you want faster experimentation and world‑class design without extra headcount, grab a slot on our calendar. We are personally onboarding the first batch of customers.

name: ISSEN
tagline: AI realtime voice companion for language learning
description: ISSEN is an AI realtime voice language app that helps you become fluent in any language. Your on-demand voice tutor is personal to you, adapting lessons and chats to your skill level, particular interests, learning style, and goals. Try it at issen.com. Conversation is essential for learning a language, yet no app provides truly free-flowing dialogue. At best, current solutions have you respond to preset messages to evaluate your pronunciation and word choice. The only effective solution for this is non-technological: a language tutor. Having a tutor can be awesome, but there are real downsides: these can be costly ($20/hour on italki on average), difficult to schedule, and have a high upfront cost (finding a tutor you like often forces you to cycle through a few that you don’t). We set out to redefine what a language learning app can be—not by building on existing solutions, but by starting with the experience of a human tutor. ISSEN is a true language tutor, accessible anytime from your computer or phone. Conversations can be as structured or open-ended as you like—dive into focused grammar lessons or have a casual chat about your day. Over time, you build a relationship with your AI tutor, which means that as they get to know you, they tailor conversations and lesson plans to your needs–but will never judge you when you make a mistake! Plus, our AI tutors are deeply multi-lingual, so if you are a Danish/Mandarin Chinese native looking to learn Gujarati, ISSEN is the app for you. Our mission is to transform language learning by delivering the connection, effectiveness, and fun that, until now, only human teachers could offer. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7sftLFew6eM We’re Mariano and Anton, co-founders who met at Stanford and share a passion for language learning. Mariano is a former crypto engineer and VC at a16z, and has dedicated years to mastering Japanese. Anton, a native Spanish, English, and Italian speaker, spent seven years at Palantir kickstarting and building Palantir LATAM and leading infrastructure as Chief Architect of Infrastructure, Security, and Hosting. If you’re serious about improving your foreign language skills—or know someone who is—you can try the app now at issen.com. The web app is live (with iOS and Android versions coming soon). Just enter your email, explore the features, and share your feedback with us at founders@issen.com.

name: item
tagline: The AI-Native CRM that works for you
description: Hey there 👋 we’re Akshay and Andrés, and we’re building item. Launch video: https://youtu.be/x8Z5QZNjdWw In a nutshell: item is the first agentic CRM, a system that does the work. A system that understands your business the way your best employee does, and works for you 24/7. If you’re building the future and still running on a 10-year-old CRM, let us show you what it feels like to run autonomously → item.app Meet Item item connects to all your customer tools, gathers and enriches context automatically, and becomes your company’s source of truth — one place where your team and your AI agents share the full picture to act autonomously. At the center is the Assistant — your sidekick for daily work. It can send follow-ups, research leads, or add deals with a simple prompt. No complex UI, drop-downs or fields, simply ask. For repeatable work, you build Agents that you can train like new hires, simply by writing documents that describe your processes. item Agents run business processes across sales, support, and account management autonomously. The Status Quo AI has changed nearly every part of work. Yet the system at the center, the CRM, hasn’t evolved. It’s manual, data incomplete, and still takes trained experts to setup and automate. We believe a CRM should know everything about your company and be able to do human-level work on your behalf, just like your best employee would. How it works: After a meeting, item helps you send a follow-up based on the notes in that meeting and your history with that customer: item searches the web to find information about any company or contact, allowing you to add deals with rich information without needing to enter fields yourself: You can build agents in item to automate any recurring human-level process by writing a simple, Notion-style document: item gives you clean, context-rich contact and company profiles with table views and Kanban boards: How companies use item Agents today to run autonomously: The team We met at AngelList, building tools to help founders start and grow companies. item comes from that same spirit — built with care, designed with intention, and shaped by a decade spent building systems of record at places like SAP, IBM, and Follow-Up Boss.

name: Janus
tagline: Evaluate AI Agents with Simulation Environments
description: Hey Everyone! We’re Shivum and Jet, the co-founders of Janus! 👋🏼 TLDR; Janus battle-tests your AI agents to surface hallucinations, rule violations, and tool-call/performance failures. We run thousands of AI simulations against your chat/voice agents and offer custom evals for further model improvement. Launch Video 💸 Why this matters A single broken AI conversation can mean: Yet most teams still test agents manually by pasting prompts into playgrounds. 🤕 The Problem Manual QA covers maybe 100 scenarios, while real users trigger millions. Generic testing platforms don’t understand your customers and can’t simulate nuanced back‑and‑forths at scale. This leaves companies with no actionable insights and blind spots that only appear after you ship. 💡 Our Solution Janus automatically: All in < 10 min. 📜 Backstory Shivum and Jet left incoming roles at Anduril and IBM, dropped out of Carnegie Mellon ML, and moved to SF to build Janus full-time. We felt this pain first‑hand while building consumer-facing agents ourselves: every new model or prompt tweak broke something in prod. We built Janus to give ourselves the “crash‑test dummy” we wished existed from day-1. 🚀 Our Ask Building or piloting an AI agent? Skip manual QA and get started in 15 minutes to see how Janus makes agent eval effortless: cal.com/team/janus/quick-chat. — Shivum & Jet (Founders of Janus) Check us out at withjanus.com. Email us at team@withjanus.com

name: Jasper.ai
tagline: Jasper is a leader in creating content using Artificial Intelligence
description: Jasper was founded in 2021, but its cofounders go back much further. 8 years ago, Dave Rogenmoser, JP Morgan and Chris Hull sat down and conceived the most wildly ambitious goal they could imagine at that point. 1. Start a company (doesn’t matter what)2. Make $6k/mo to support their families 3. Most importantly, their friendship and commitment to each other would supersede 1 and 2.After shutting down two failed SaaS companies, they finally started a digital marketing agency. Their first client was doing SEO for a porta-potty rental company for $300/mo. After a year, they pivoted and spent the next three years building a course company teaching all things marketing. Courses businesses are great for learning, but hard to scale. They decided they wanted to build a SaaS company and they wanted to go big. That meant we needed to get into YC. The team decided to apply to Y Combinator because they wanted the foundation and acceleration to make a scaleable SaaS company happen. They initially applied with a company called Proof and were rejected because the idea was too small - more of a feature than a product. After the rejection, they kept building. They spent six months growing Proof and applied again. This time they had gone from zero to $175K in 10 months and refined their vision. The combination of vision plus traction landed them the spot in YC.“YC was amazing,” Dave Rogenmoser later recounted, “I can’t recommend it enough.” The team worked hard and kept dreaming of where the company was headed. The week after Demo Day they had raised $2.2M at a $12M cap. They moved to Austin, TX, and were on their way. Over the next two and a half years, the company worked hard on Proof, but in the fall of 2000, growth stalled and that feature-not-a-product challenge persisted. The founders made the hard decision to pivot and start anew with an AI company. Jasper went from launch to unicorn in 18 months. It’s one of the fastest-growing startups of all time, but like many startups the journey was longer than people may think. The founders first started working together eight years before Jasper’s Series A. They knew they wanted to build something together but took a few attempts to get to a product with traction. They started a marketing agency, then a learning platform, then a personalization and social proof company before hitting upon AI. When they saw generative AI in 2020, they knew how impactful it could be. With marketing roots, they had first-hand experience with the pain that many marketers feel keeping up with content demands and creating enough testing variations to produce good insights. The idea for Jasper started as a way to create and test ad variations, but they quickly saw how impactful it could be in long-form content and expanded into the AI platform you know today. Today Jasper is an AI platform built for growing businesses. When you give Jasper a prompt, it selects from a range of language models the right starting model for your use case, then it can add context about your business, reliable source citations and more current data to improve outputs for your business. In 2022, Jasper added an extension enabling users to call upon its AI-assistance anywhere they create. In 2023, Jasper introduced a collection of major new features for business users. Jasper now has tools for SEO, generating images, checking for plagiarism, collaboration and more. It is becoming an integral part of how businesses create content that resonates with their target audiences. With its ever-growing suite of features and capabilities, Jasper continues to make content creation easier than ever before for nearly a hundred thousand businesses and growing. For executives and content teams at growing companies, It’s impossible to keep up with content demands. The number of channels, campaigns and topics companies need to cover proliferate every year. It’s no longer enough to have a blog. You must have a blog, podcast, growing newsletter and social audiences across a half dozen different sites and communities. Every content creator knows the pain of staring at a blank page on a deadline, and this pressure leads our teams to chase word-count and produce lower quality content. Jasper helps businesses leverage generative AI to accelerate the writing process so they can give their teams more breathing room for their ideas. Jasper is working to become the AI platform that helps the world’s businesses unlock their best ideas. We want growing companies to be able to tap AI that matches their brand voice, creates the best possible outcomes for their use cases, and can be called upon in all the places their teams create. Along the way we’re working to help businesses transform the way they operate, learn to leverage AI responsibly, and grow.

name: Founder
description: TL;DR - jo is a personal AI that runs on your Mac and a private cloud machine we host for you. It knows your life, improves itself autonomously, chats via  Telegram and WhatsApp, and never shares your data. Less "chatbot" and more "a second brain that does things." https://youtu.be/7u6vxYF44rI Hey YC! We're Kevin and Pradeep, co-founders for a decade and consumer builders since the mid-2000s. For the last few years, we’ve iterated on an experience that’s zero compromise. It’s easy to use and gives consumers control over their privacy. 🧠 jo knows you. jo improves over time as it learns your preferences, kids' schedules, travel habits, and work context. It reviews its own notes nightly and gets better as you use it. We’ve found the continuity over time to be immensely useful. 🏠 Your machine, your data. Powerful cloud inference runs the best available models (OpenAI, Anthropic, Grok, Kimi with more on the way) on top of cloud providers that are barred from training on your data. ⚡ 10x faster, everywhere you already are. Voice on your Mac. Text & voice on  Telegram and Whatsapp. Consistent  memory and personality, 24/7. What it does 👉 Sign up for the beta: askjo.ai (first 100 people skip the onboarding and can download ASAP) Other things jo does Native mobile app, multiplayer (shared family/team assistants), trusted purchases, grocery shopping. Built on open-source roots. We stand on the shoulders of giants and contribute back. 👉 Sign up for the beta: askjo.ai (first 100 people skip the onboarding and can download ASAP) Please share your feedback. Ask us anything.

name: Founder
tagline: Co-founder of Dex. Prev Math & Stat at Harvard, quant/ML researcher at Jump Trading, IBM Research, QuantCo, Meta.
description:
Hey! We’re Kevin and Regina from Dexterity. Today, we’re launching Dex—the fastest interface for co-working with AI, right inside your browser. TLDR: AI that works with you, not just for you. 10x your workflows without breaking your flow. Try Dex (Beta) → getdexterity.com Four weeks before Demo Day, we pivoted to build for ourselves. Most AI web agents today are “set-and-forget” chats—you hand them off and check back later. But what about workflows you actually want to drive? Navigating platforms, moving data, making quick decisions—these moments demand instant, context-aware models that work with you, not just for you. Imagine using Operator to order DoorDash—a frustratingly back-and-forth relay with no real-time visibility. That same friction exists for work tools like Jira, GSuite, and GitHub, where users need instant feedback and responsive action, not slow AI handoffs. That’s why we’re taking a Cursor (vs. Devin) approach—for the browser. Modern work is a juggling act of 15+ tabs, software bloat, and constant UI friction, just to complete a single task. The real bottleneck isn’t just automation—it’s context switching. Traditional solutions force users to memorize shortcuts (appeals to devs, but leaves everyone else behind), while AI tools make you adapt to them instead of living right where you work. Why not turn your browser itself into an intelligent personal workspace? With simple voice and text, Dex enhances familiar interfaces, automates mundane actions, and understands your intent. Think of it like Spotlight Search meets AI automation + personal expert for everyday apps. We’re building Dex for power users, founders, students, and curious explorers—because that’s who we are. Join our waitlist → Get notified when Dex goes live on the Chrome store.
Follow us on Twitter → Stay updated on latest features.
Discord → Get to know us more personally + brainstorm cool ideas. Drop dream features to regina@getdexterity.com and let us know what you think!

name: JustAI
description:
tldr: We’re building an AI-first marketing tech stack for enterprises. We are on a mission to rethink growth by turning content into conversations. Our core product allows companies to auto-refresh content on growth channels like emails using reinforcement learning, artificial intelligence, and causal inferences. Companies have 10X their speed of experimentation, and have seen stat sig engagement lift with our technology. 1. Traditional A/B testing is slow. It can take 3-6 months to run one test because of organizational decision-making and lack of ML infrastructure. 2. Content and copy selection is based on vibes. 3. Prioritization of the biggest opportunities is decided by the loudest voice in the room. 1. Enrich CRM with ML powered personalization 2. Auto-refresh content on the fly. Auto-tune underperforming content with newly generated dynamic content in real time 3. Use insights from infinite testing to predict the next best action. 1. Check out justwords.ai and book a demo for your use case (email, sms, push, landing page titles). 2. Connect us to your growth & marketing teams. Book a 30 min slot with us or drop us an email at founders@justwords.ai. When we were heading Growth at Twitter, we saw the power of copy changes. When we tweaked a push notification from “Elon tweeted” to “Elon just tweeted”, we gained 800K additional users. We never underestimated the power of copy since that day. The company funded it as a big investment in the difficult years when Twitter’s growth was tanking. However, each change would take 3-6 months to pick, test, approve, and ship. We also didn’t know how to use those learnings systematically to prioritize the next copy change. We started building internal tools to automate this and gained 20% additional user growth. We stumbled upon similar problems and “vision docs” at Pinterest. We knew we were staring at money on the table. We quit our jobs to build just words. Today, lifecycle marketing is built on manual effort — copywriters brainstorming variants, data scientists running A/B tests, and marketers trying to make sense of disconnected dashboards. Every campaign is a one-off experiment. In the world we’re building, AI-marketers will handle this entire loop — strategy, creation, optimization, and learning — with humans acting as orchestrators, not operators. The world will shift from “humans using tools” to humans teaming with AI agents.
And in doing so, we’ll make personalization not just scalable, but truly human again — every message relevant, every experience intelligent, every brand empathetic at scale.

name: JustPaid
tagline: Billing Automation for B2B that actually works.
description: JustPaid.ai is reimagining B2B accounts receivable workflows, stepping away from rigid legacy CPQs, and entering a new era of finance efficiency, connecting CRO and CFO in one place. Within seconds, the platform converts fully executed complex sales contracts into dynamic revenue records (customer names, billing parameters, customer notes) - where the journey of each invoice is timely generated, payments are reconciled automatically, and human-like collections workflows guide the cash gently into your bank account. Here’s a preview: https://youtu.be/baNem5HVYQQ JustPaid.ai excels in managing complexity and effortlessly handling consumption, tiered plans, renewals, price discounts, and custom SLAs. Integrations to support your workflows with: JustPaid.ai is positioned to help any growing enterprise. Ready to step into the future and consolidate the tools you are using for billing? Email sales@justpaid.ai Story on the founders: JustPaid.ai is the brainchild of Anelya Grant, Daniel Kivatinos, and Vinay Pinnaka. Anelya is a two-time female founder with a profound understanding of accounting and finance. She led a top accounting practice for tech startups in Silicon Valley for 15 years. Daniel is a two-time founder and the co-founder of DrChrono, the first YC healthcare company and one of the YC’s top healthcare exits. Vinay is an exceptional software talent with expertise in ML and AI and years of experience building complex billing architecture. Accounts Receivable is a massive multibillion-dollar market, with around 12%—15% projected growth over the next few years. With companies focusing on improving cash flow management, JustPaid.ai helps them step into the future and reduce the time spent on billing per month, payment reconciliation, and collections.

name: Kabilah
description: ❌ Problem: The Impossible Task of Patient Handoff Picture this - you are an inpatient bedside nurse starting your shift, entrusted with the care of five patients due to commonplace staffing shortages. As you get a rapid-fire handoff from the previous nurse, you jot down critical information for each patient on a paper report sheet. Throughout your shift, you’re continually juggling urgent patient care demands, reading and updating your scribbled notes, attempting to communicate with inundated physicians, and charting/navigating data-dense notes within the EHR system. By the end of your shift, it is impossible to communicate all of the pertinent information about each patient in a rapid-fire handoff. This perpetual cycle leads to increased medical errors, increased patient length of stay, and redundant inter-clinician messaging. ✅ Solution: Intelligent Patient Advocates We automate and simplify shift change by replacing paper report sheets with an AI-powered advocate for each patient. Each Patient Advocate is a digital report sheet with: (Please reach out to founders@kabilah.com for a demo!) 🏆 Founding Team We are Sarah & Umar! Sarah - Stanford MS/BS in Computer Science (AI), 2022 Mayfield Fellow, second hire at EngFlow, previously founded nationwide nonprofit organization AWARE. Umar - Recent cancer survivor :) Stanford BS in Computer Science (AI), MS in Management Science & Engineering, and 2023 Mayfield Fellow. 👋🏼 Our Ask We're signing on our next two healthcare systems. If you can help make introductions to Hospital Execs or Nursing Leadership, please reach out to founders@kabilah.com!

name: Kaelio
tagline: The context layer your data agents need
description: Hey everyone! We’re Luca and Andrey, the cofounders of Kaelio. TL;DR: Healthcare organizations are drowning in dashboards they can't act on - or worse, flying blind because they lack the resources. Kaelio unifies clinical, operational, and financial data for hospitals, health systems, and health plans - giving them an AI copilot that answers business-critical questions, flags operational risks, and recommends actions to improve clinical outcomes and financial performance. We’re currently onboarding our first customers. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rBqZDSekjbo Healthcare organizations are sitting on a goldmine of data from EHRs, finance, procurement, staffing, and many more systems. But most of it stays locked away in silos: disconnected, messy, and hard to use. Decision-makers face two constant struggles: they’re either (1) drowning in dashboards they can’t tactically act on, or (2) flying blind because they don’t have the resources to analyze that data. Simple, but critical questions like: often take days or weeks to answer. Sometimes, they never get answered at all. This leads to missed financial opportunities, operational inefficiencies, and worse patient outcomes. Hiring teams of analysts is expensive. Waiting for consultants is too slow. Yet the need for fast, reliable insights is greater than ever. Kaelio acts as a copilot for healthcare decision-makers. Ask questions in plain-English and get answers in seconds. Features: We’re working with forward-thinking hospitals, health systems and health plans feeling like they’re drowning in dashboards that fail to inform day-to-day decisions - or simply lacking the resources to leverage their data. If that sounds familiar, we'd love to talk. Visit https://kaelio.com/ or reach out to luca@kaelio.com Because healthcare decisions shouldn’t be guesswork.

name: Kanava AI
tagline: Your First AI Sales Hire for Wholesale Distributors
description:
Industrial Sales teams are underwater. Here’s why - and what we’re doing to fix it. Hi everyone! We’re Smit and Vik - the founders of Kanava AI Problem and Opportunity: The wholesale distribution industry is massive: $9T+ in annual revenue. 410,000+ businesses. 6 million+ employees. And yet — sales teams are drowning. A third of the workforce is over 55, and replacements aren’t coming fast enough. (“Most of my sales reps are retiring in the next 2-3 years, I have NO idea what I’ll do after” - a medium sized electrical goods distributor from Indiana we met) Reps spend less than 30% of their time selling. The rest? Answering repetitive calls (“What’s the price of this SKU?” “Which warehouse has it?”), digging through systems, and manual follow-ups. Over 25% of inbound calls never reach a human — lost revenue, lost trust, lost time. This isn’t just inefficiency. It’s operational drag at scale — and it's only getting worse. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=s1NVVxRnLL8 How’re we solving it: We’re building Kanava AI, a voice-based AI agent designed for wholesale distributors. It handles repetitive tasks like answering stock, price, and order status calls using live ERP data and logging field notes hands-free, drafting follow-ups, updating the CRM all automatically. It’s like a junior rep-in-training. It starts with the basics, but improves quickly by learning from the company’s best performers. Over time, Kanava takes on more: •⁠ ⁠Managing consultative sales calls
•⁠ ⁠Identifying upsell opportunities in real time
•⁠ ⁠Surfacing coaching insights from rep conversations
•⁠ ⁠Re-engaging dormant accounts with timely, data-backed outreach And by the time these reps are ready to retire, Kanava is ready to fill the void they leave. Most AI tools in this space fail because they try to do too much, too early, and distributors are famously late adopters. We’re taking a more deliberate path: start with low-leverage work, prove value, and scale responsibility as the system earns it. Our Team Vik (CTO) and Smit (CEO) know each other since freshman year of college at UC Berkeley. Both of us studied CS, and worked as AI engineers at various startups before deciding to build our own thing and join YC. Smit: Built incident resolution agents at a series B observability startup called Observe. Previously worked at Abnormal Security and Salesforce. Vik: Built voice agents for numerical data entry and hands-free navigation for a data management platform called Uncountable. Previously worked at Uber and Amazon. We’re both outsiders to the wholesale distribution industry, but we’re listening hard and learning fast. What made us really want to work together was being on the same ultimate frisbee team in the senior year of college - that team was a disaster, we lost every single game we played by huge margins, but despite that, we had a ton of fun and stuck together! Our Ask If you know any distributors, field sales heavy businesses or IMR’s (Independent Manufacturer Reps), we’d appreciate a connection at smit@kanava.ai or vikhyath@kanava.ai. We’ll make their lives a lot easier and buy you a coffee ☕🤎

name: kapa.ai

We’re Finn and Emil, the co-founders of kapa.ai. 👋 The idea to build kapa.ai initially came from a friend that runs a developer-facing company complain about the amount of “read the f’ing manual” questions they get and how much of their engineers’ time that costs. The bread and butter of what we’ve been building is a super scalable and state-of-the-art crawling infrastructure to ensure high quality data is kept up to date and compatible with LLMs. Here are a few examples of how companies are deploying kapa.ai: In addition to reducing support, we’re also helping companies identify gaps in their documentation and products by analyzing the answers of their kapa.ai bot. We met +6 years ago in London and ended up doing two master’s degrees together: (1) Finance at London School of Economics, and (2) Computer Science at Imperial College London. While building kapa.ai, Finn (CTO) leans towards doing most of the engineering, bringing his experience from building low-latency trading systems at Bloomberg, although he also did sales at Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan. Emil (CEO) handles most of the business, bringing his experience as an Engagement Manager at McKinsey & Company, but also ships, which he learnt while building applied AI systems at startups. We’d love to help your company deploy a kapa.ai bot (takes less than 24hrs!). And we’re always looking to connect with folks at developer-facing companies if you’d be up for making an intro. Reach us at founders@kapa.ai or sign up for access here and mention “YC Launch” Thank you so much.

name: Kashikoi
tagline: Simulation Engine for Benchmarking AI Products
description:
Hey YC!
We are Tim and Aaksha - cofounders of Kashikoi. Kashikoi is a simulation engine to benchmark GenAI Agents. We generate CPU friendly world models that autonomously interview agents and generate deep behavioral assessments. Building high-performing AI agents is becoming increasingly complex. Teams face many challenges: Despite growing interest and investment, most solutions rely heavily on prompt engineering, public benchmarks, or surface-level observability. These approaches often mislead more than they inform, creating a false sense of progress. Today’s “LLMs” are adaptive systems which run test time adaptation loops behind that tiny “Thinking…” blinking on the screen. We are building a scalable version of test time adaptation and inference scaling a.k.a World Models that bring the power of these techniques to you. Simply put you can simulate highly customized benchmarks, diverse data and align your evaluations while maintaining all of these for the long run, all without writing prompts! Our world models unlock automatic prompt optimization and detecting regression test staleness as fun side effects. LLM based Systems are getting smarter and so should you using our World models! Check out our simulation engine adaptively interviewing RAG Agents and multi-turn evaluation in action here. Sign up for our waitlist at getkashikoi.com, or email us at founders@getkashikoi.com if you or someone you know wants:

name: Kenley
tagline: AI Agents for Advisory & Financial Services.
description:
Kenley is the AI platform built for consulting firms. We help firms power critical workflows—from drafting proposal decks to scaling due diligence research—so consultants can focus on excellence. Demo Here Consulting firms sit on a wealth of insight, but it’s buried in decks, transcripts, and distributed tools. Most AI platforms ignore the unique workflows of services firms. The result? Hours wasted searching for precedent, rewriting the same slides, and missing what your firm already knows. Kenley WorkStreams connects your firm’s expertise directly to the work your teams deliver. Leverage your firm’s expertise Kenley connects siloed information across decks, meeting notes, and other operational systems into one unified search experience. Consultants can instantly surface precedent from past work, find relevant subject-matter experts, or reference similar deliverables. No more hunting through SharePoint or pinging colleagues for context. It’s the fastest way to apply what your firm already knows to the work in front of you. Uncover conversational insights Every consulting project generates valuable insight through expert interviews, internal meetings, and client working sessions, but most of it goes unstructured and unused. Kenley captures and synthesizes these conversations to surface themes, recommendations, and supporting evidence. Results are traceable to source, so nothing is lost in translation. Teams can move from raw transcripts to cited insight in minutes, not hours. Generate high-quality decks Kenley helps consultants draft slides, summaries, and deliverables that reflect the firm’s voice and standards. Whether building a market landscape, summarizing client input, or structuring a SoW, WorkStreams saves time without sacrificing quality. It works with your templates and draws on your internal precedent to produce outputs that are 80% ready out of the box. Consultants spend less time formatting and more time thinking. Enterprise-Grade Data Security and Access Controls The Kenley platform is built with enterprise-grade data security at its foundation; robust access controls, role-based permissions, and strict data isolation. Kenley never uses your data for external training or 3rd-party storage. You stay in control of your information, with the assurance that every output respects firm policies and client confidentiality. Request a Demo
Request a demo to learn how leading firms are using Kenley to drive consulting excellence with AI.

name: Kestrel AI
description:
Hey everyone, we’re Raman and Evan from Kestrel 👋

TL;DR: Kestrel helps teams using Kubernetes detect, investigate, and fix issues without manual firefighting. It monitors cluster health and security 24/7, finds root causes, and generates ready-to-apply YAML fixes - so incidents get resolved before you even reach for your laptop.

Launch video: https://youtu.be/HA2YziEBMVw Ask: If your team runs Kubernetes, we’d love to hear from you! You can reach out to us at raman@usekestrel.ai or book a demo here. Or get started in minutes with a 14-day trial! Running Kubernetes at scale is chaos.

As founding engineers on the Kubernetes Security team at Illumio, we saw both startups and large organizations struggle with its complexity and scale. For example, network policies are so tricky that many teams default to allowing everything, and even basic ops tasks like debugging failed rollouts mean spending hours digging through logs and events just to find where things went wrong.

Over time, this complexity compounds and clusters begin to drift - configs diverge, policies break, and “temporary” workarounds become production. And when something goes wrong, engineers are buried under alerts and reports from disconnected tools, trying to debug blind at 3 a.m. Kestrel is redefining how teams manage Kubernetes with the first agentic platform that unifies ops and security. It replaces manual triage with autonomous investigations and human-in-the-loop remediations - so issues that used to take hours to debug can be understood and fixed in seconds. 💬 AI Chat Assistant: Investigate any Kubernetes issue across all of your clusters in plain English - skip the complex queries and context switching, and get answers with ready-to-apply YAML fixes from a single point of command. 🚨 Incident Response: Kestrel continuously monitors your clusters to detect incidents, trace root causes, and generate YAML fixes that can be applied with a single click. It's like having a dedicated on-call SRE team - without the burnout or headcount. 🌐 Topology Map: Visualize your Kubernetes infra with classified intra-cluster, cross-cluster, and external traffic overlaid in real time. ⚠️ Risk Assessments: Launch a swarm of specialized AI agents that discover vulnerabilities and provide exact remediation steps across network security, RBAC, privilege escalation, container image security and more. Validate fixes with a single click. Raman and Evan were founding engineers on Illumio’s Kubernetes team, where they built distributed systems to secure clusters for Fortune 500 companies.

Raman previously worked on ML systems research at Sky Lab and BAIR. Evan was a security researcher during his Master’s in Computer Science, focusing on the Signal Protocol.

name: Keyframe Labs

name: Keylika
tagline: Redefining the Standard of Care for Iron Deficiency Anemia
description: Keylika is combining its metallodrug synthesis technology with optimized delivery methods to obtain improved clinical outcomes. Our first product is a novel iron drug in a wearable skin patch with significantly improved safety, efficacy and tolerability to treat Iron Deficiency Anemia (IDA). When approved, Keylika’s proprietary transdermal iron will be the world's first prescription iron patch. Iron Deficiency Anemia (IDA) is a hugely understated, highly prevalent problem with subpar treatments. Iron deficiency (ID) affects 30 million people in the US alone and about 1.6 billion worldwide. Current treatments include oral iron supplements which are typically associated with low bioavailability and poor tolerability due to mild to severe gastrointestinal (GI) side-effects (constipation, nausea, stomach discomfort, diarrhea, etc.) often leading to patient non-compliance. Also, a significant number of patients just do not absorb iron taken orally.  The second line of treatment with intravenous (IV) iron infusions is more invasive, riskier, costlier and requires hospitalization due to the risk of anaphylactic shocks. Our first product is a new small molecule (new molecular entity), an iron-containing drug designed for transdermal delivery by a wearable skin patch: NistuFe. It is a safe, highly bioavailable iron complex with higher efficacy. A therapeutically relevant dosage is administered through the skin directly into the systemic circulation, thereby circumventing the gastrointestinal tract and all associated adverse effects. Thus, we combine the benefits of both orals and IV iron, and without their respective downsides: a drug suitable for self-administration with no GI side effects and no required hospitalization that yields a superior treatment outcome from the convenience of a patient’s home. This is slated to be a potentially best-in-class, breakthrough therapy. Our preliminary proof-of-concept in vivo studies in rats have demonstrated excellent results. At our core is a platform synthesis technology we have developed for synthesizing mixed ligand metal complexes. We are using this platform to generate a range of drug candidates for treating therapeutic conditions in the fields of hematology, pain and inflammation, metabolic disorders, infectious diseases and oncology. We then administer these drugs using proven delivery technologies, to effect the best clinical outcomes. Positioned at the intersection of drug development and drug delivery, we customize the molecular structure of the drug and its formulation to optimize the therapeutic delivery per clinical requirements. Our first indication being menorrhagia, our primary target population is women of child-bearing age in the US who suffer from severe to moderate ID, which has an estimated prevalence of 6 million. A wide range of other patient groups adds up to a potential 30 million users in the US alone. 1.     If you know anyone in your social circle suffering from Iron Deficiency, we would love to talk to them! 2.     If you are connected to biopharma companies with interests in the hematology space, could you please introduce us? Please follow us on LinkedIn! Questions? Feel free to reach out: founders@keylika.com We develop metallodrugs for unmet medical needs.

name: Khoj
description: Hi folks! We’re Saba and Debanjum from Khoj and we’re working hard to make personal AI accessible to everyone, including your mum and grandad 😇 Khoj is an open-source, personal AI application that helps you extend your digital brain. It's built by a team that scaled personal AI assistance for enterprises to 10s of millions of daily active users at Microsoft. You can try Khoj on WhatsApp at +1 (848)-800-4242 Computing capabilities have advanced at a staggering rate over the last few decades, but our individual capabilities have stagnated. Petabytes of data have been digitized, and yet our tools to engage with them hover somewhere around fuzzy search. While we were building more advanced tools for enterprise assistance, we realized that there weren’t good equivalents to helping individuals across work and life. I've to make sense of what is covered by my health insurance plan in some outdated PDF, track down stray notes I made during my 100th daily standup meeting, or look for that one email I sent back in 2018 to Carl. The current set of tools for this just doesn’t cut it. All the while, the surface area occupied by our digital interactions is making us less happy and more distracted. With Khoj, we're creating your open-source, superhuman companion to tackle these challenges. Right now, you can use Khoj’s desktop app to search and chat with your existing notes and docs (see below!). Instead of awkwardly keyword searching, you can have a natural conversation with your knowledge base. Hundreds of technical, advanced knowledge workers are already using this. It supports fully offline chat and search. See the docs. But technical capability shouldn’t limit access to great digital assistance. That's why we're rolling out the ability to access Khoj over WhatsApp as well. We’re building your companion with two key principles in mind: Debanjum and Saba met while building and scaling enterprise personal AI assistance at Microsoft. They became fast friends while bonding over hiking in the PNW and hacking on fun side projects together. Saba has studied CS @ UIUC, built resilient data pipelines for high-frequency trading at Two Sigma, and developed the ML infrastructure at a YC biotech startup. Debanjum is a published researcher with a double Masters in Math and CS at BITS and Dartmouth respectively. He helped create Hillhacks, an Indian hacker conference that's been running self-sustained for 9+ years. Tell us what you'd like out of your own personal superhuman companion at team@khoj.dev. Chat with Khoj on WhatsApp at +1 (848)-800-4242 or by using the QR code below. You can also send it voice messages! 🎙️

name: Kino AI
tagline: Kino is an AI media manager and video editor for humans and agents.
description: Hey! Wes and Luke here! We’re building software for filmmakers at Kino AI. Luke Igel and I actually started our entrepreneurial journey as amateur documentary filmmakers. We met as freshman at MIT in 2018 and have been friends ever since. In November 2020, we began working together on a feature-length documentary film about the history of MIT and technology in the United States. It’s called MIT:Regressions. Making Regressions inspired us to build tools that help professional storytellers. Imagine watching this footage and transcribing all of the slated information by hand. People have to do this - and much more - to organize the troths of footage that go into a movie, or a commercial, or a trailer, or a YouTube video, or a…you get the point. The process of manually logging this metadata is expensive and time consuming, and mistakes can be catastrophic. Kino automatically infers metadata and uses it to label and organize footage, saving hours every day for assistant editors. We transcribe audio and “describe” the video and turn an unstructured mountain of footage into a useful index. And it all happens locally on the editor’s machine. Our first feature lets users use natural language to find specific moments within their entire footage library. Check out this demo video! We’re running pilots with production companies, creative agencies, and corporate video teams. Please contact sales: sales@trykino.com Connect with us! -Wes and Luke

name: Kirana AI
description:
Prateek and I are building a new full-stack manager for grocery stores. Growing up, in the summers I worked at my family's grocery store, and saw first hand the challenges my father went through on a day to day basis. My family's store was started over 100 years ago, and with this industry experience, Prateek and I are able to offer new insights.
Overview The Problem: Operating an independent grocery store means surviving on razor-thin margins. What We Built- Our Team- **The Vision-**
We chose to work in this space to help local grocers thrive in the AI age and be on the forefront of innovation. Our cameras are just the first step- by combining POS, ordering, and camera data, we can automate every back-office process in the store, allowing them to achieve their optimal margins.
**Our Ask-**
If you know any grocery or retail store owners who’d love a tireless 24/7 assistant to cut shrink, boost safety, and maximize sales, please loop us in!

name: Klaimee
tagline: Insurance for the agentic world. You deploy agents, we cover you.
description:
AI agents can now execute financial transactions, access sensitive data, and take real-world actions, but there is no insurance, no certification, and no clear liability framework for when they fail. This is the same gap that existed with cyber risk in the early 2000s, before a $15B+ insurance market emerged. We’re at that same inflection point with AI. The signals are already there: AI-related incidents are being carved out of traditional liability coverage, lawsuits are growing rapidly, and enterprises are still under pressure to deploy AI. The result is a fast-growing, unaddressed risk layer. We saw this firsthand. We were building AI agents for claims processing: handling payments and decisions inside regulated environments. Every customer asked the same question: who is liable when the agent gets it wrong? There was no answer. That’s when it became clear: this infrastructure has to exist before enterprise AI can scale. Ines had already built insurance products from zero at SafetyWing, including carrier relationships and underwriting frameworks. We realized we were uniquely positioned to build the certification and insurance layer for AI agents - so we did. Every transformative technology needs a trust layer before it can scale.
The internet needed cybersecurity. Cloud computing needed compliance and audit standards. AI is next. Today, AI agents can act but they can’t be trusted at scale. There is no standard for how they’re evaluated, no clear liability when they fail, and no infrastructure to make them safe to deploy in critical environments. If we succeed, the liability and certification framework we build won't just protect enterprises, it will make AI itself safer, more accountable, and more auditable at a global scale. We are not just selling insurance. We are building the infrastructure that allows the agentic world to exist.

name: Koyal
description:
https://youtu.be/j5t8kEuZpSc?si=q_Jcbcnanrjl4h-N TL;DR Create cinema that feels real. Koyal captures emotional tone from your audio, turning emotion into cinematic scenes with consistent characters and settings. You can storyboard, direct, and edit every scene to perfection. Filmmakers, musicians, and creators spend weeks to months scripting, shooting, and editing or pay $100,000+ to agencies to get something that feels right.

Even with new AI tools, you’re still wrestling with prompting, uncanny performances, and inconsistent characters. Koyal lets you speak your story once, and watch it come to life.
We capture the emotional tone in your voice or script and translate it into expressive, cinematic performances with consistent characters, settings and storylines. You can storyboard, reshoot, and refine each scene naturally, like directing your own crew: no complex prompts, no model wrangling, no editors needed.

Just your voice, your script, and your vision. Demo Video Upload your script or record your voiceover: Koyal understands the emotion, pacing, and intent behind every line. Design your characters: Create an AI character or safely place yourself in the film using CHARCHA: our Character Captcha protocol that ensures your likeness is used with verified consent. Craft your world: Create or find real-life locations to shoot your movie in, add assets and choose art styles (animated, realistic, etc.) Auto-generate cinematic scenes: complete with consistent characters, environments, and shot logic. Storyboard and edit visually: adjust lighting, camera angle, or emotion directly with simple directions like “make it more hopeful” or “change the setting to night.” Export your finished film: ready to share across YouTube, socials, or investor decks. We’ve battle-tested Koyal across music, media, and startups. Who We Are We’re Mehul and Gauri Agarwal, a brother-sister team obsessed with reimagining how stories are told.

We are a team from MIT, CMU and Meta with experience building in AI video. Koyal evolved directly from our research published at Neurips 2024. Koyal is inspired by how Pixar builds stories : audio first, visuals second.
We aim to replace the camera with intelligent AI systems that understand how to translate human intent into cinematic storytelling. You no longer need cameras, expensive sets and set pieces, or prompts: just your voice and imagination. Try it now at koyal.ai We can’t wait to see what you create.
Mehul & Gauri - kookoo

Hey, We’re Darren and Ronit of Kura. We just built the State of the Art for Browser Agents and wanted to share our learnings. First, here’s what 87% accurate Browser Agents look like:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=G62KRiW4ses\ Browser What? Browser Agents. Generalized agents that can perform a task on the internet via computer vision and reading the DOM.

How does it work TL;DR our agent is a multi agent system composed of a planner, executor and critic engaged in debate over each action. The agents have varying degrees of vision and HTML DOM context. For more information please refer to https://trykura.com/technical-deep-dive.

Why is it better

This debate architecture allows it to self heal and backtrack succeeding in several tasks other agents currently fail at. It's also largely model agnostic allowing a user to lower costs by up to 90% when compared to Claude’s Computer Use by swapping in cheaper models with slight impacts to accuracy.

Why does this matter We believe, because of many of the companies here, there will be more agents than humans on the internet in 10 years. However, the internet today is made for humans. Significant functionality is locked behind UIs which don’t have APIs. That’s where generalized browser agents come in. Our browser agent converts any series of UIs into an API ready for consumption by the next generation of agentic companies.

Kyber’s LetterGen helps companies streamline their notice creation process, removing tedious overhead and minimizing risk from claimants suing companies for acting in bad faith. Out of the box, Kyber’s LetterGen comes with:

name: Laminar
tagline: Understand why your AI agent breaks. Iterate fast to fix it.
description: Hey everyone, Robert from Laminar (YC S24) here. The entire observability space was built for request-response LLM apps. Nobody was building for Agents that run for 40 minutes and fail in ways you can't reproduce. So we did. Most observability platforms just collect data and wait until you go look at it. And when you do, you get a tree of spans. You click into one, read it, go back, click the next one, try to hold the context in your head. For a simple LLM call, that's fine. For an agent that ran for 30 minutes and made 200 decisions, it's practically useless. We built Laminar's tracing to give you as much information as quickly as possible. Our trace timeline and reader mode lay out the agent's reasoning and actions as a clean, readable feed. You open a trace and immediately see what the agent did, what it was thinking, and where things went wrong (we also capture and show application level exceptions). For browser agents, we record full browser sessions synced with traces. You literally see what the agent saw at every step → https://laminar.sh/shared/traces/16fcd540-9583-8bcb-f8e0-8b15ec92c58d. If a trace is too complex to parse visually, you can chat with it: ask questions about what happened in natural language, instead of manually digging through hundreds of steps. We take into account the whole context of the trace, not just a single span. We can confidently say that we have the best tracing DX on the market. It’s one line of code to integrate. Laminar SDK auto-patches vast majority of AI frameworks and SDKs, including Claude Agent SDK, AI SDK, LiteLLM, Browser Use, Stagehand, OpenHands SDK, and much more. We are the only platform that traces Claude Agent SDK sub-agents. When Claude delegates work to sub-agents, you get full visibility into that entire chain, not just the top-level call. Here’s an example of a trace https://laminar.sh/shared/traces/3f9219d0-8691-daf7-836a-8874ca4c1d9f. This is the feature we wished existed when we were building agents ourselves. When your agent fails 15 minutes into a run, the normal workflow is: restart from scratch, wait for it to reach the same state, hope it reproduces the failure. That's insane. Laminar starts a local dev server that connects to our platform. You can run your agent directly from our UI, and when a run fails, you go to the exact step where it went wrong. You tweak your prompt or tool definitions right in the UI. And you rerun from that step — with full context preserved. Here's how it works under the hood: our tracing SDK sits right before the LLM call boundary. When you rerun from a step, we mock all the LLM calls that happened before that point, replaying their original responses. This means the agent walks through its prior steps instantly without actually calling any LLMs and spending any tokens — and crucially, it properly restores external state along the way. If your agent was controlling a browser, the browser gets back to the exact page and DOM state. If it was working in a sandbox, the sandbox is restored. By the time execution reaches your breakpoint, everything — conversation history, tool state, external environment — is exactly as it was. You tweak your prompt, hit rerun, and the agent picks up from there with the real world intact. Here’s a demo of Laminar debugger. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=iSw8MM6tRvY In development, you care about individual runs. In production, you have thousands of runs and the question changes — it's no longer "why did this run fail," it's "what's going wrong across all my runs and how often." That's what Signals solve. You write a short natural language description of what you want to detect — something like "agent gets stuck in a retry loop" or "user gets frustrated and rephrases their request." Laminar runs this against every trace, extracts matching events, and then clusters them into patterns. So instead of manually sampling traces hoping to spot trends, you get a structured view of what's actually happening. We use this ourselves to monitor our own agents, and it catches things we'd never find by skimming through traces. All of your data lives is accessible with built-in SQL editor (both in UI and API). You can run arbitrary queries against your traces, spans, and events — build custom dashboards, do ad-hoc analysis, or bulk-create datasets from production traces. Those datasets plug directly into our evals pipeline, so you can run evaluations on real production data instead of synthetic test cases. Leading agent companies like Browser Use, OpenHands, Rye.com, Alai and many more use Laminar in production. Laminar is fully open source and extremely fast (written in Rust). Self-host it anywhere or use our managed platform (https://laminar.sh). Our ask: 🔗 https://laminar.sh

name: LanceDB
tagline: Open-source, serverless vectordb for production-scale generative AI
description: 🙋 Are you building a generative AI app using an LLM or API? Are you working on a new recsys, a modern search engine, or a new analytical tool for unstructured data? Good news: you no longer have to struggle with Pinecone’s high cost, over the top complexity, or data privacy concerns. 🚀 LanceDB is a free and open-source vector database that you can run locally or on your own server. It’s lightning fast and is easy to embed into your backend server. Check out our github repo or pip install lancedb to get started. For a quick demo, you can take a look at our sample youtube transcript search app. We’ll be adding a lot more soon. For more details on the API, take a look at LanceDB docs. As we spoke to builders of ML/AI applications, a common refrain from users was struggling to get services like Pinecone even running. After a while, we realized that the retrieve-filter-hydrate workflow was often time a big bottleneck in productivity and app latency. So we put our heads together. I was one of the original co-authors of the pandas library. Lei was a core-contributor to HDFS and led ML infrastructure at Cruise. Using our experience building data/ML tooling, we’ve built something totally new. We’ve reimagined vector search from the ground up for better developer productivity, scalability, and performance. LanceDB is backed by Lance format — a modern columnar data format that is an alternative to parquet. It’s optimized for high speed random access and management of AI datasets like vectors, documents, and images. We then added our own Rust implementations of a number of SOTA ANN-index algorithms to support low-latency vector search. These indices are SSD-based and can easily scale wayyyy beyond memory. What’s more, LanceDB allows you to store and filter other features along with vectors. Our users have been able to replace 3–4 different data stores with LanceDB alone and achieve a speedup at the same time. Since our launch, the community has added LangChain support. Our integration for LlamaIndex is also under review. Our current focus is building a TypeScript implementation with a native-level experience. Currently, we provide a python package called lancedb which is pip installable and delivers a great local workflow. Beyond what we’re working on right now, here’s what you can expect: To get started with LanceDB head over to our repo. If you have questions, feedback, or want help using LanceDB in your app, don’t hesitate to drop us a line at contact@lancedb.com.
